sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Alexis, presents with a 6-month history of numbness and tingling in her right hand, with symptoms becoming more persistent over the last month.
She reports experiencing pain, especially when typing on the keyboard and using a mouse.
The pain is relieved by rest and ibuprofen.
She denies pain at night, and it is not affecting her sleep.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: I prescribed ibuprofen 600 milligrams four times a day for pain.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has pain on wrist extension and flexion, a positive Tinel's sign, and less grip strength on the right hand.
The numbness is most pronounced in the thumb, index finger, and middle finger.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: I suspect carpal tunnel syndrome based on the patient's symptoms and physical examination findings.
Problem list: Carpal tunnel syndrome; active.
Plan:
I recommended a wrist splint to be worn at night, and I want the patient to continue with ibuprofen 600 milligrams four times a day for pain.
I ordered an EMG nerve conduction study to assess the nerve impingement in the wrist.
We will re-evaluate the patient based on the test results, and may consider physical therapy or surgery if necessary.
Follow-up: The patient will return for a follow-up visit to discuss the EMG nerve conduction study results and determine the next course of treatment.
